name: Aspire Bank
description_short: FDIC-insured bank serving North Dakota, Minnesota, and Arizona with personal and business banking, lending, insurance, and tax services.
description_long:
Aspire Bank is a regional FDIC-insured bank headquartered in Fargo, North Dakota, operating across three states: North Dakota, Minnesota, and Arizona. The bank positions itself as a full-service financial institution rather than a single-service provider, integrating banking, lending, insurance, and tax/accounting services under one roof. This integrated model distinguishes it from traditional banks that focus exclusively on deposit and lending products.

The bank offers comprehensive personal banking solutions including checking accounts, savings accounts, and lending options. For business customers, Aspire Bank provides business checking, business savings, customized lending, SBA loans, and equipment financing. Their product suite also includes 7-month CDs with competitive rates (3.65% APY as of January 27, 2026, requiring $25,000 minimum). Beyond banking, they operate Aspire Insurance for personal and business coverage, plus tax and accounting services for both individual and business clients.

Aspire Bank emphasizes personalized service and quick decision-making, particularly for small business clients. Customer testimonials highlight their responsiveness to growing businesses and knowledgeable staff. The bank's positioning as a specialist in business lending and real estate financing (acquisition, equipment, working capital) suggests a deliberate focus on the small-to-medium business market rather than consumer banking broadly. Their integration of insurance and tax services creates a potential competitive advantage for busy business owners seeking consolidated financial guidance.

As a regional bank limited to three states, Aspire Bank has geographical constraints that limit national accessibility. While the website emphasizes personalized service and business focus, specific lending rates, fees, account minimums for checking/savings accounts, and approval timelines are not disclosed, requiring customers to contact the bank directly. The $25,000 minimum for their advertised CD rate may be prohibitive for some consumers.
pros: FDIC-insured deposits with full faith and credit backing
Integrated banking, insurance, and tax services under one organization
Specialized business lending with customized solutions and SBA loans available
Competitive 7-month CD rate at 3.65% APY (as of January 2026)
Online and mobile banking platform for account management
Local specialist teams with emphasis on quick decision-making for business clients
Serves multiple states (North Dakota, Minnesota, Arizona) expanding regional availability
cons: Limited geographic availability to only three states restricts national accessibility
Specific checking/savings account fees, minimums, and interest rates not disclosed on website
High minimum balance ($25,000) required for advertised CD rates excludes many consumers
No clear information about approval timelines or lending decision processes
Limited detail on business loan rates, terms, or qualification requirements online
best_for: Small business owners in North Dakota, Minnesota, or Arizona seeking integrated banking and lending
Business customers needing quick lending decisions and personalized service
Individuals with $25,000+ seeking competitive CD rates in the bank's service regions
Business owners wanting consolidated banking, insurance, and tax services from one provider
diagnosis: Aspire Bank is best suited for small business owners and entrepreneurs in its three-state service area seeking personalized lending decisions and integrated financial services. The primary caveat is geographic limitation and lack of transparent pricing information online, requiring direct contact for specific rates, fees, and qualification details.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
